routes.reason: assess processor / sub-processor and international-transfer recipient risk
automated decision-making / novel technology warrants a dedicated AI risk assessment
map the end-to-end personal-data flows and lineage for the processing

processing_purpose.title: Processing Description & Purpose
items: Purpose: rank customers by predicted churn and recommend a retention offer via the marketing platform
Data controller: the bank; processing operated by Retail Marketing with a model vendor as processor

data_inventory.title: Personal Data Inventory & Categories
items: Personal data: customer identifiers, product holdings, transaction aggregates, channel engagement
Special-category proxy: inferred financial-vulnerability indicator flagged as sensitive; volume ~2.1m data subjects

legal_basis.title: Legal Basis, Necessity & Proportionality
items: Lawful basis: legitimate interests for churn scoring, with a documented balancing test; opt-out honored
Necessity/proportionality: feature set minimized; sensitive proxy gated behind an Article 9 substantial-public-interest / consent condition

data_sharing.title: Data Sharing, Recipients & International Transfers
items: Recipients: analytics vendor (processor) under a data-processing agreement; no onward sale
International transfer to a third-country vendor covered by standard contractual clauses + transfer risk assessment

retention.title: Retention & Data Minimization
items: Scores retained 13 months then deleted; training data pseudonymized after model refresh
Retention aligned to the records-retention schedule; deletion routine tested

security.title: Security & Technical/Organizational Measures
items: Encryption in transit and at rest; role-based access; vendor SOC 2 Type II reviewed
Pseudonymization of training data; access logging and monitoring in place

data_subject_rights.title: Data Subject Rights & Transparency
items: Transparency: privacy notice updated to describe scoring and the retention offer; no solely-automated decision with legal effect
Rights: access, rectification, erasure, objection to profiling, and opt-out supported operationally

mitigations.title: Risk Mitigations & Safeguards
items: Sensitive proxy access restricted and separately approved; human review before any adverse retention action
Bias/fairness evaluation scheduled; opt-out suppression enforced at scoring time

assessment_scope.title: Assessment Scope & Trigger
assessment_scope.processing_name: AI-assisted customer churn and next-best-action model
assessment_scope.note: Scope and DPIA trigger for context; this assessment does not authorize the processing.

privacy_risk_assessment.title: Privacy Risk Assessment
privacy_risk_assessment.note: Deterministic indicator of risk to data subjects' rights and freedoms to inform human sign-off; not a decision on the processing or a lawful basis of record.

recommendation.title: Recommendation for Sign-off
recommendation.note: Residual privacy risk assessed High on documented factors. Recommend DPO and senior review with the mitigations tracked to completion and prior consultation with the supervisory authority (GDPR Art 36) considered before any go-live sign-off decision. Recommendation only — not a decision, lawful-basis setting, or filing.

approvals.title: Approvals & Sign-off
approvals.note: Draft is queued for sign-off; obtaining these approvals is the human step.

sources_citations.title: Sources & Citations
sources_citations.note: Every asserted evidence item is mapped to an approved source above.

standing_note_limitations.title: Standing Note / Limitations
standing_note_limitations.text: Draft privacy impact assessment for human sign-off only. This assessment records the processing description, personal-data inventory, legal basis, sharing, retention, security, data-subject rights, risks, and mitigations with source citations and a risk-based recommendation; it makes no approval of the processing, sets no lawful basis of record, files nothing with a supervisory authority, writes no system of record, and has not been sent or submitted. Every regulated privacy decision and sign-off remains with the authorized Data Protection Officer / privacy adjudicator.

standing_note: Draft privacy impact assessment for human sign-off only. This assessment records the processing description, personal-data inventory, legal basis, sharing, retention, security, data-subject rights, risks, and mitigations with source citations and a risk-based recommendation; it makes no approval of the processing, sets no lawful basis of record, files nothing with a supervisory authority, writes no system of record, and has not been sent or submitted. Every regulated privacy decision and sign-off remains with the authorized Data Protection Officer / privacy adjudicator.
